name: Turing Machine Mk II
description: Random looping sequencer built on a 16-bit shift register. Big knob sets probability of bit-flip per clock, locking into repeating melodies at extremes. Backpack header drives Volts, Pulses, Voltages and Vactrol Mix expanders.

controls.name: Big knob (probability)
controls.description: Sets probability of inverting the first bit each clock. Noon is 50% (fully random); extremes lock the loop; sides slip occasional notes.
controls.detail: noon: random · 3 o'clock / 9 o'clock: slips occasional notes · 5 o'clock: locked loop · 7 o'clock: locked loop at 2x length

controls.name: Length switch
controls.description: Rotary switch selecting loop length in steps. Combines with big knob CCW position to double the effective length.
controls.detail: 2 · 3 · 4 · 5 · 6 · 8 · 12 · 16 steps · doubled when big knob at 7 o'clock

controls.name: Write switch
controls.description: Momentary 3-way. Up injects a 1 into the first bit each clock (fills LEDs); down injects a 0 (clears LEDs). Centre is normal.
controls.detail: up: force 1 · centre: normal · down: force 0 · acts on clock edge while held

controls.name: Clock knob
controls.description: Rate of the internal clock. Unused when an external clock is patched into CLOCK IN (input normalled to internal).
controls.detail: ~1 Hz to audio rate · square wave · disconnected when CLOCK IN patched

controls.name: Scale knob
controls.description: Attenuates the main CV output. From silence up to several octaves of swing. Quantizer recommended for 12-tone work.
controls.detail: 0V up to ~5V (multiple octaves) · attenuator on stepped DAC output

inputs.name: CLOCK IN
inputs.description: External clock. Rising edge advances the shift register one step. Square wave or trigger works; audio-rate clocks produce noise-like textures.
inputs.normalizedTo: internal clock set by Clock knob

inputs.name: KNOB CV
inputs.description: CV input summed with big knob position. Modulate probability with LFO/envelope for jumps between locked and random states.

outputs.name: CV OUT
outputs.description: Main stepped DAC output. Sum of last 8 bits of the shift register scaled by the Scale knob. 256 discrete levels before scaling.

outputs.name: PULSE OUT
outputs.description: Gate output tapped from the shift register — goes high on clock steps whose register bit is 1. Useful as a rhythmic accent trigger that tracks the locked melody.

outputs.name: NOISE OUT
outputs.description: White-noise source that feeds the internal shift register. Independent output for external use as noise source or sample-and-hold fodder.

behaviors.name: Shift-register mechanics
behaviors.description: On each clock, bits shift right and bit 0 is re-filled: a copy of bit 16 XORed with noise, with the mix set by the big knob. Extremes yield pure feedback (locked loop) or pure noise (random).

behaviors.name: Loop locking
behaviors.trigger: big knob fully CW or CCW
behaviors.description: Fully CW locks the 16-bit pattern into a loop of the length set by the switch. Fully CCW locks into a 2x-length pattern. Small knob twists away from extremes sprinkle mutations.

behaviors.name: Write-switch editing
behaviors.description: Lets the player edit a locked loop live. Up gradually fills the register with 1s (rising melody); down with 0s (dropping). Tap on specific beats to carve phrases out of a random stream.

behaviors.name: Backpack expander bus
behaviors.description: 16-bit parallel bus exposes every register step to expanders. Volts (5 pots), Pulses (11 outs), Voltages (8 faders), Vactrol Mix (matrix) all tap the same bus. Daisy-chainable.

behaviors.name: Audio-rate clocking
behaviors.description: Feeding an oscillator into CLOCK turns the module into a chaotic oscillator / noise source. CV OUT produces metallic grit; PULSE OUT becomes a rhythmic subharmonic.

patchIdeas.name: Locked generative melody
patchIdeas.patch: External clock drives a locked 8-step loop, CV OUT goes through a quantizer to an oscillator, and nudging the big knob CCW introduces slow mutations.
steps: Set the LENGTH switch to 8.
Turn the BIG KNOB fully clockwise so the loop is locked (5 o'clock).
Set the SCALE knob to about noon (~2-3 V swing).
Patch your external clock (e.g. Pamela's) → Turing Machine CLOCK IN.
Patch Turing Machine CV OUT → quantizer IN; set the quantizer to minor pentatonic.
Patch quantizer OUT → VCO V/OCT.
Patch Turing Machine PULSE OUT → envelope TRIG; envelope → VCA CV; VCO → VCA → output.
To evolve the phrase, nudge the BIG KNOB just CCW of 5 o'clock — the LEDs will flicker and notes swap one at a time.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CV OUT — stepped 0 to ~5 V pitch CV into a quantizer, then to a VCO's V/OCT.
patchIdeas.hear: An 8-note in-key melody that repeats exactly while locked, with the PULSE OUT punching envelopes on the high notes. Nudge the big knob CCW and single notes begin to swap out, mutating the phrase.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Turing Machine:
* Length: 8 · Big knob: fully CW (locked)

Clock:

Quantizer:

- Clock (Out) c> Turing Machine (CLOCK IN)
- Turing Machine (CV OUT) p> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Turing Machine (PULSE OUT) g> Envelope (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Probability modulation
patchIdeas.patch: A very slow LFO into KNOB CV drifts the module between fully random and fully locked without touching the panel.
steps: Set the BIG KNOB to noon (fully random).
Set the LENGTH switch to 8.
Patch a slow LFO (triangle, ~0.05 Hz / 20-second cycle) → Turing Machine KNOB CV.
Patch your external clock → Turing Machine CLOCK IN.
Patch Turing Machine CV OUT → VCO V/OCT (optionally through a quantizer).
Patch Turing Machine PULSE OUT → envelope TRIG for rhythmic accents.
Listen for the LFO peaks — the big knob 'virtually' sweeps to the extremes, locking the loop for a few bars before unlocking again.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CV OUT — stepped pitch CV that cycles between random and locked melody, feeding the VCO.
patchIdeas.hear: The module breathes between chaos and order: random burbling when the LFO is near zero, and a settled repeating melody when the LFO peaks positive or negative. Great for unattended live drift.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Turing Machine:
* Big knob: noon

LFO:
* Rate: 0.05 Hz triangle

Clock:

VCO:

- LFO (Out) >> Turing Machine (KNOB CV)
- Clock (Out) c> Turing Machine (CLOCK IN)
- Turing Machine (CV OUT) p> VCO (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Write-switch melodic editing
patchIdeas.patch: Lock the loop CW, then hold the WRITE switch DOWN on strong beats to carve phrases out by forcing zeros into the register.
steps: Set the LENGTH switch to 16.
Turn the BIG KNOB fully clockwise to lock the loop.
Patch your clock → Turing Machine CLOCK IN.
Patch Turing Machine CV OUT → VCO V/OCT (optionally via a quantizer).
Patch Turing Machine PULSE OUT → drum module TRIG.
Let the loop play until you hear a phrase you like.
Hold the WRITE switch DOWN on strong beats to inject 0s — the drum thins out as register bits clear.
Flick WRITE UP on other beats to inject 1s and rebuild density. Release to centre to re-lock whatever pattern you landed on.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CV OUT — locked stepped pitch CV, editable in real time; PULSE OUT — gate that thins/densifies as you edit.
patchIdeas.hear: A locked 16-note line that you sculpt live: holding WRITE DOWN drops notes out of the melody and sparsens the drum, WRITE UP fills it back in. Performance-ready subtractive editing.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Turing Machine:
* Length: 16 · Big knob: locked CW

- Clock (Out) c> Turing Machine (CLOCK IN)
- Turing Machine (CV OUT) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Turing Machine (PULSE OUT) g> Drum (Trig) // thins out as register clears

patchIdeas.name: Bassline + drum pulse
patchIdeas.patch: One external clock drives both the kick and the Turing Machine — quantized CV OUT plays a locked bassline while PULSE OUT adds hats.
steps: Set the LENGTH switch to 8.
Turn the BIG KNOB fully clockwise to lock the loop.
Set the SCALE knob to ~1 o'clock (roughly 2 octaves of swing).
Patch your external clock → Turing Machine CLOCK IN and mult the same clock → Kick drum TRIG.
Patch Turing Machine CV OUT → quantizer IN (set to a minor scale).
Patch quantizer OUT → bass VCO V/OCT; bass VCO → VCA → mixer.
Patch Turing Machine PULSE OUT → hi-hat TRIG.
Adjust SCALE to taste — lower for a narrow bass range, higher for octave jumps.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CV OUT (via quantizer) — 8-step locked bassline into bass VCO; PULSE OUT — hi-hat trigger.
patchIdeas.hear: A tight 8-step techno groove: steady kick on every beat, repeating bass riff underneath, and hats punching in on register highs. One clock, one module, a whole rhythm section.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Turing Machine:
* Length: 8 · Big knob: CW locked

Clock:

Quantizer:

- Clock (Out) c> Turing Machine (CLOCK IN)
- Clock (Out) c> Kick (Trig)
- Turing Machine (CV OUT) p> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> Bass VCO (V/Oct)
- Turing Machine (PULSE OUT) t> HiHat (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Volts expander 5-step sequencer
patchIdeas.patch: With a Volts expander attached and the loop locked, each of the 5 pots becomes a step of a tuned mini-sequencer.
steps: Connect the Volts expander to the Turing Machine backpack header (power off first).
Set the LENGTH switch to 5 so the loop length matches the number of Volts pots.
Turn the BIG KNOB fully clockwise to lock the loop.
Patch your clock → Turing Machine CLOCK IN.
Patch Volts OUT → VCO V/OCT.
Patch Turing Machine PULSE OUT → envelope TRIG; envelope → VCA CV; VCO → VCA → output.
Tune each of the 5 Volts pots by ear while the loop plays — it is a locked 5-step tuned sequencer.
Nudge the BIG KNOB slightly off lock to reorder the steps on the fly.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Volts (Out) — stepped pitch CV, one value per pot, into the VCO V/OCT.
patchIdeas.hear: A locked 5-note phrase where every note's pitch is under your direct fingertip control via the pots — like a tiny hand-tuned step sequencer glued to the Turing Machine's shift register.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Turing Machine:
* Big knob: CW locked

Volts:
* 5 faders set 5 CV steps

Clock:

- Clock (Out) c> Turing Machine (CLOCK IN)
- Volts (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Turing Machine (PULSE OUT) g> Envelope (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Pulses expander euclidean-like rhythms
patchIdeas.patch: The Pulses expander exposes 11 gate outputs, one per register bit — route a few to different drums for repeating euclidean-flavoured grooves.
steps: Connect the Pulses expander to the backpack header (power off first).
Set the LENGTH switch to 16.
Turn the BIG KNOB fully clockwise to lock the loop.
Patch your clock → Turing Machine CLOCK IN.
Patch Pulses OUT 1 → Kick TRIG.
Patch Pulses OUT 2 → Snare TRIG.
Patch Pulses OUT 4 → HiHat TRIG.
Patch Pulses OUT 7 → Perc TRIG.
If you dislike the pattern, nudge the BIG KNOB CCW one click to swap a bit and re-lock — repeat until the groove lands.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Pulses (Out 1/2/4/7) — four 0/+5 V gate streams into four drum trigger inputs.
patchIdeas.hear: A locked 16-step drum pattern where each output fires on its own subset of steps — kick, snare, hats and perc sit in different-but-related rhythms like a hand-drawn euclidean groove.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Turing Machine:
* Length: 16 · Big knob: CW locked

Pulses:
* 11 pulse outs from register bits

- Clock (Out) c> Turing Machine (CLOCK IN)
- Pulses (Out 1) t> Kick (Trig)
- Pulses (Out 2) t> Snare (Trig)
- Pulses (Out 4) t> HiHat (Trig)
- Pulses (Out 7) t> Perc (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Voltages expander dual CV
patchIdeas.patch: The Voltages expander's 8 faders define two CV sequences — one for pitch, one for filter — moved live to re-harmonize.
steps: Connect the Voltages expander to the backpack header (power off first).
Set the LENGTH switch to 8 to match the 8 faders.
Turn the BIG KNOB fully clockwise to lock the loop.
Patch your clock → Turing Machine CLOCK IN.
Patch Voltages CV A → VCO V/OCT (optionally via a quantizer).
Patch Voltages CV B → filter CUTOFF.
Tune the faders by ear: upper row for melody, lower row for brightness per step.
Turn Scale or the filter input attenuator to taste — each fader is now a note + a timbre, playing in lock-step.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Voltages (CV A) — stepped pitch CV to VCO; Voltages (CV B) — stepped modulation CV to filter cutoff.
patchIdeas.hear: A locked 8-step melody where every note also has its own filter brightness. Sliding a fader instantly re-tunes or re-colours that step without stopping the sequence.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Turing Machine:
* Length: 8 · Big knob: CW locked

Voltages:
* 8 faders · 2 CV outs

- Clock (Out) c> Turing Machine (CLOCK IN)
- Voltages (CV A) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Voltages (CV B) >> Filter (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: Noise source utility
patchIdeas.patch: Ignore the sequencing entirely and use NOISE OUT as a dedicated white-noise source for hats and drones.
steps: Leave CLOCK IN, KNOB CV and CV OUT unpatched.
Patch Turing Machine NOISE OUT → filter IN.
Set the filter to bandpass or high resonance for a hi-hat tone.
Patch an LFO → filter CUTOFF for a shimmering sweep, or leave static.
Patch an envelope → VCA CV; filter OUT → VCA IN → mixer.
Trigger the envelope from any clock for hats/cymbals/noise bursts.
patchIdeas.signalOut: NOISE OUT — ~10 Vpp white noise into the filter, then into a VCA.
patchIdeas.hear: A clean white-noise source — perfect for closed hats, wind textures or sample-and-hold fodder — with the rest of the Turing Machine sitting silent.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Turing Machine:

- Turing Machine (NOISE OUT) -> Filter (In)
- LFO (Out) >> Filter (Cutoff) // hi-hat-style perc
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Audio-rate chaos oscillator
patchIdeas.patch: Feed a VCO square into CLOCK IN — CV OUT becomes metallic digital grit tracked by the VCO pitch.
steps: Set the BIG KNOB to noon (random) and LENGTH to any position.
Patch a VCO's square output (audio rate, ~100 Hz–1 kHz) → Turing Machine CLOCK IN.
Patch Turing Machine CV OUT → filter IN.
Patch an envelope → VCA CV, filter OUT → VCA IN → mixer.
Play the driving VCO's V/OCT from a keyboard or sequencer — CV OUT now tracks its pitch as inharmonic noise.
Sweep the BIG KNOB toward either lock point for metallic resonant tones; leave at noon for full noise.
patchIdeas.signalOut: CV OUT — audio-rate stepped signal (treated as audio here), 0 to ~5 V, into the filter.
patchIdeas.hear: A gritty, digital noise voice that follows the driving VCO's pitch — great for FX, crash cymbals, and growly bass-noise hybrids. Locks into metallic tones at the knob extremes.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Turing Machine:
* Big knob: noon

VCO:
* Wave: square · audio rate

Filter:

Envelope:

VCA:

- VCO (Square) c> Turing Machine (CLOCK IN)
- Turing Machine (CV OUT) -> Filter (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- Filter (Out) -> VCA (In)

patchIdeas.name: Dual Turing duet
patchIdeas.patch: Two Turing Machines share one clock but run different lengths and lock states — a polymetric two-part duet.
steps: Set Turing A: LENGTH = 8, BIG KNOB fully CW (locked 8-step loop).
Set Turing B: LENGTH = 12, BIG KNOB fully CCW (locked 2×-length = 24-step loop).
Patch one external clock, mult it → Turing A CLOCK IN and → Turing B CLOCK IN.
Patch Turing A CV OUT → quantizer IN 1; quantizer OUT 1 → VCO 1 V/OCT.
Patch Turing B CV OUT → quantizer IN 2; quantizer OUT 2 → VCO 2 V/OCT.
Set both quantizer channels to the same scale (e.g. minor).
Trigger envelopes for both voices from the shared clock or from each module's PULSE OUT.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Turing A CV OUT and Turing B CV OUT — two stepped pitch CVs into one shared quantizer, feeding two VCOs.
patchIdeas.hear: Two locked melodies of 8 and 24 steps playing simultaneously in the same key — they only align every 24 beats, so the duet keeps finding new harmonic combinations without ever truly drifting.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Turing A:
* Length: 8 · Big knob: CW locked

Turing B:
* Length: 12 · Big knob: CCW locked (2x = 24)

Quantizer:

- Clock (Out) c> Turing A (CLOCK IN)
- Clock (Out) c> Turing B (CLOCK IN)
- Turing A (CV OUT) p> Quantizer (In 1)
- Turing B (CV OUT) p> Quantizer (In 2)
- Quantizer (Out 1) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Quantizer (Out 2) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
